items.title_original: The Cost of Correctness in “Real-Time” Systems Like Kafka and Spark
items.title_neutral: The Cost of Correctness in “Real-Time” Systems Like Kafka and Spark
items.excerpt: "Real-time" with Kafka and Spark is controlled delay, not instantaneity. Kafka batches for durability; Spark processes streams as micro-batch jobs with watermarks and checkpoints. Exactly-once requires the sink to cooperate. The right question isn't whether a pipeline is real-time it's which latenc…
